headline: Waste Management Crisis as 8 Landfills Approach Capacity
context: Eight of Malaysia 14 operational sanitary landfills projected to reach capacity by 2028 with no new sites approved.

cards.big: 8 of 14 sanitary landfills will reach capacity by 2028 with no replacements
cards.sub: Malaysia still sends 82% of waste to landfill versus 4% in Japan

cards.big: Malaysia generates 38,000 tonnes of solid waste daily recycling only 31%
cards.sub: Recycling improved from 24% in 2020 but remains far below 60% target

cards.big: Waste-to-energy in Ladang Tanah Merah processes only 1,200 tonnes daily
cards.sub: Single WTE handles just 3% of national waste despite RM1.1 billion cost

cards.big: Illegal dumps increased from 1,200 to 1,850 between 2022 and 2025
cards.sub: Local councils spend RM320 million annually cleaning up illegal waste

cards.big: Landfill capacity tests whether waste management gets ahead of the crisis or reacts after
Eight landfills approaching capacity. Waste management needs circular economy incentives, not just more dumping sites.
